text: Minimalist shoes improve 5-km time trial performance compared to conventional shoes in trained runners.
Minimalist shoes improve running economy during sub-maximal running compared to conventional shoes.
Reduced shoe mass accounts for approximately 79% of the improved running economy seen with minimalist shoes.
Minimalist shoes decrease stride length and increase stride rate compared to conventional shoes.
Improvements in 5-km time trial performance with minimalist shoes are associated with improvements in running economy at 15 km/h.
Switching to minimalist shoes does not substantially change footfall pattern, as nearly all runners maintained a rearfoot strike.
